description: Phase 1 — Foundation & Profile System: Scaffold Next.js 15 project, set up Prisma/SQLite database, build profile system (create, select, switch), deploy to Vercel.

userStories.title: Project scaffolding and tooling
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a properly configured Next.js 15 project so that all subsequent work has a solid foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js 15 (App Router) project initialized with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
Path alias @/* → ./src/* configured in tsconfig.json
ESLint configured and passing
Vitest configured with a sample passing test
Playwright configured with a sample passing E2E test
npm run dev starts the dev server at localhost:3000
npm run build completes without errors
npm run test runs Vitest tests
npm run test:e2e runs Playwright tests
npm run lint runs ESLint
.gitignore includes node_modules, .next, .env, dev.db, playwright-report
Install dependencies: zod, date-fns, lucide-react, @anthropic-ai/sdk
iPad Safari viewport meta tag with viewport-fit=cover in root layout
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use create-next-app with --app --typescript --tailwind flags. Install Vitest with @vitejs/plugin-react and happy-dom. Install Playwright with npx playwright install.

userStories.title: Database schema and Prisma setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database schema defined so that all features can persist data.
acceptanceCriteria: Prisma installed with @prisma/client and prisma as devDep
prisma/schema.prisma uses datasource sqlite with url from env DATABASE_URL
Profile model: id (cuid), name (String), avatarId (String), createdAt, updatedAt

Session model: id (cuid), profileId (FK to Profile), language (String de/en), mode (String daily/free), startedAt (DateTime), completedAt (DateTime nullable), totalPoints (Int default 0), exerciseCount (Int default 0)

ExerciseResult model: id (cuid), sessionId (FK), profileId (FK), language (String), category (String), topic (String), exerciseType (String), question (String as JSON), userAnswer (String as JSON), correctAnswer (String as JSON), isCorrect (Boolean), pointsEarned (Int default 0), timeSpentSec (Int default 0), createdAt (DateTime)
Achievement model: id (cuid), key (String unique), nameDE (String), nameEN (String), descriptionDE (String), descriptionEN (String), iconId (String), category (String), threshold (Int)
ProfileAchievement model: id (cuid), profileId (FK), achievementId (FK), earnedAt (DateTime); unique constraint on profileId+achievmentId
Streak model: id (cuid), profileId (FK), language (String), currentStreak (Int default 0), longestStreak (Int default 0), lastPracticeDate (String); unique constraint on profileId+language
Indexes on: Session(profileId, language), Session(profileId, startedAt), ExerciseResult(profileId, language, topic), ExerciseResult(sessionId), Streak(profileId, language)
Cascade delete on Profile: deleting a profile deletes sessions, results, profileAchievements, streaks
npx prisma migrate dev runs successfully against local SQLite
Prisma client singleton at src/lib/prisma.ts (reuse in dev to avoid hot-reload connection issues)
.env file with DATABASE_URL=file:./dev.db
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use standard Prisma with SQLite for now (not Turso adapter — that's for production). Use cuid() for id generation.

userStories.title: Achievement seed data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reference achievement data seeded so that the gamification system has badges to award.
acceptanceCriteria: Seed script at prisma/seed-achievements.ts using Prisma client
Seeds streak badges: streak_3, streak_7, streak_14, streak_30, streak_100 with appropriate nameDE/nameEN/descriptions/iconId/category/threshold
Seeds mastery badges: grammar_star, spelling_ace, reading_pro, writing_hero
Seeds milestone badges: first_session, ten_sessions, hundred_sessions, perfect_5, both_languages, level_5
Seeds special badges: early_bird, night_owl, weekend_warrior
npm run seed:achievements runs the seed script (add to package.json scripts)
Seed is idempotent — uses upsert so running multiple times doesn't create duplicates
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use prisma.$transaction with upsert for idempotency. Configure prisma seed in package.json.

userStories.title: Standardized API response helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reusable API response utilities so all endpoints return consistent formats.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/api-response.ts exports successResponse(data, status?) returning NextResponse with { success: true, data }
src/lib/api-response.ts exports errorResponse(code, message, status?) returning NextResponse with { success: false, error: { code, message } }
Error code constants exported: INVALID_INPUT, NOT_FOUND, FORBIDDEN, CONFLICT, SERVER_ERROR
Helper validateBody<T>(request, zodSchema) that parses JSON body against schema, returns parsed data or calls errorResponse with INVALID_INPUT
Unit test: successResponse produces correct shape
Unit test: errorResponse produces correct shape
Unit test: validateBody returns parsed data for valid input, returns error response for invalid
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is moved ahead of US-006 because the profile API routes depend on these helpers.

userStories.title: Profile API routes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CRUD API routes for profiles so the frontend can manage them.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/profiles returns all profiles as { success: true, data: profiles[] } with id, name, avatarId
POST /api/profiles creates profile with body { name, avatarId }, validates with Zod (name: 2-30 chars, required; avatarId: required string), returns created profile
GET /api/profiles/[id] returns single profile or 404
PATCH /api/profiles/[id] updates name and/or avatarId with Zod validation
DELETE /api/profiles/[id] deletes profile and all associated data (cascade)
All routes use successResponse/errorResponse helpers from api-response.ts
Integration test: full CRUD cycle — create, read single, read all, update, delete, verify deleted
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Next.js App Router route handlers (route.ts files). Use the validateBody helper from US-010.

userStories.title: Profile creation page
userStories.description: As a student, I want to create my profile with a name and fun avatar so the app knows who I am.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /profiles/new with a name input field and avatar selector
Avatar selector shows 8-10 animal options (fox, owl, dragon, cat, dog, bear, rabbit, unicorn, panda, tiger) as colorful icons using lucide-react or emoji fallback
Name field validates: required, 2-30 characters, shows inline error
Submit button creates profile via POST /api/profiles
After creation, redirects to /[profileId] using router.push
Responsive: works on iPad portrait (768px) and desktop
Large tap targets (min 48px) for avatar selection on touch devices
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use client component with useState for form state. Use fetch to call the API.

userStories.title: Profile picker (home screen)
userStories.description: As a student, I want to see a 'Who is practicing?' screen when I open the app so I can select my profile.
acceptanceCriteria: Root page / shows 'Who is practicing?' heading
Fetches and displays all existing profiles as cards (avatar + name)
Tapping a profile card navigates to /[profileId]
Add new profile button/card navigates to /profiles/new
If no profiles exist, shows only the 'Create your first profile' prompt with link to /profiles/new
Responsive: 2-column grid on iPad, 3-column on desktop, 1-column on phone
Loading state while profiles are fetched
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Can use server component with async data fetching from Prisma directly, or client component with fetch.

userStories.title: Profile-scoped layout with navigation
userStories.description: As a student, I want to see my name and avatar in the app header so I know I'm in my profile, and navigate between sections.
acceptanceCriteria: Layout at /[profileId]/layout.tsx wraps all profile-scoped pages
Header shows profile avatar (small) + name on the left, points/level placeholder on the right
Bottom navigation bar with icons for: Home, Practice, Progress, Badges
Switch profile link/button returns to /
ProfileProvider React context at src/contexts/ProfileContext.tsx provides current profile data to child components
Responsive: bottom nav on mobile/iPad (<1024px), sidebar on desktop (>=1024px)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Fetch profile data in layout server component, pass to client ProfileProvider. Use lucide-react icons for nav.

userStories.title: Profile home dashboard (placeholder)
userStories.description: As a student, I want a home page after selecting my profile that shows where to start practicing.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /[profileId]/page.tsx is the profile home dashboard
Shows welcome message 'Hallo, [name]!' with the profile avatar
Two large CTA buttons: 'Daily Practice' and 'Free Practice' (link to placeholder pages or # for now)
Placeholder sections for streak display, level/points, recent activity showing 'Coming soon'
Responsive layout — cards stack on mobile, side-by-side on desktop
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use the ProfileProvider context to get the current profile name and avatar.

userStories.title: GitHub repo setup and initial commit
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the code pushed to GitHub so it is version-controlled and ready for Vercel deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: Git initialized with git init
All project files committed with descriptive initial commit message
Remote added: git remote add origin git@github.com:darkbloomy/Marc-EN-DE.git
Code pushed to main branch
CLAUDE.md updated with project commands and architecture overview
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Vercel deployment is a manual step done by the developer after push. This story just handles git setup and push.
